step.title: Command Type
step.goal: Determine command type (standalone/wrapper/agent-specific) and plan structure. This shapes everything about how the command is built and used.
step.note: THIS IS THE KEY DECISION. Commands are simpler than skills but the type choice still matters for maintainability and clarity.

substep.title: Analyze Requirements
substep.action:
Based on discovery, analyze the command's nature:
- Does it need its own workflow logic? → standalone - Does it just invoke a skill? → wrapper - Is it specific to one agent? → agent-specific
Key questions: - How complex are the instructions? - Does an existing skill do most of the work? - Should this be available globally or to one agent?

substep.title: Determine Type
substep.ask:
Based on what this command does, which type fits best?
**standalone** - Self-contained instructions - Has its own workflow or logic - Doesn't rely on an existing skill - Examples: /commit, /github, /sync
**wrapper** - Thin shell around a skill - Primarily invokes another skill - Minimal additional logic - Examples: /pr-review → pr-review skill
**agent-specific** - Only for one agent - Scoped to one agent via the config's `destination` - Only that agent's namespace gets it - Examples: /prompter/verify-compliance
Note: Agent-specific can be standalone OR wrapper.

check.action: Plan the instruction structure: - Does it need a workflow with numbered steps? - What rules should govern its behavior? - Does it need to execute scripts?
Identify the target skill: - Which skill does this wrap? - How should $ARGUMENTS be passed? - Any pre/post processing needed?
Identify the owner agent: - Which agent owns this command? - Why should it be agent-specific?

substep.title: Present Architecture

section.title: Standalone Commands
section.description: Self-contained commands with their own logic and instructions. May include workflow steps, rules, and script execution.

when-to-use.rule: Command has significant logic of its own
No existing skill covers this functionality
Logic is simple enough to not need skill checkpoints

section.structure:
command-config.yaml (build descriptor): - type: command, id, file_name, destination, includes
instructions.md: - frontmatter: description (+ optional argument-hint, agent) - body: <command> with <instructions>, optional <workflow>, <rules>

section.title: Wrapper Commands
section.description: Thin wrappers that primarily delegate to an existing skill. Minimal logic - just invoke the skill with arguments.

when-to-use.rule: An existing skill does the heavy lifting
You want a quick shortcut to trigger the skill

section.structure:
command-config.yaml (build descriptor): - type: command, id, file_name, destination, includes
instructions.md: - frontmatter: description (+ optional argument-hint) - body: <command> with a single <action> to invoke the skill, passing $ARGUMENTS

section.title: Agent-Specific Commands
section.description: Commands that only appear for a specific agent. Can be standalone or wrapper - the key is restricted availability.

when-to-use.rule: Command only makes sense for one agent

rules.rule: If a skill exists that does this → wrapper
If command needs its own logic → standalone
If only one agent should use it → agent-specific
When in doubt, standalone is safer (more explicit)

example.name: Standalone: /commit
example.context: Creates git commits with proper messages.
example.config: Type: standalone Has workflow: yes (analyze, plan, execute) Has rules: yes (never add co-author, specify files)
example.rationale: No skill to wrap. Needs its own workflow logic. Includes specific rules for commit behavior.

example.name: Wrapper: /pr-review
example.config: Type: wrapper Wraps: pr-review skill Logic: just delegate with $ARGUMENTS
example.rationale: The pr-review skill has all the complexity. Command is just a convenient entry point.

example.name: Agent-Specific: /prompter/verify-compliance
example.config: Type: agent-specific (standalone) Agent: prompter Visibility: only Percy sees this command
example.rationale: Only makes sense for the Prompter agent. Other agents don't need compliance verification.
